{{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705}} |Shaku = Shikinaisha ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}}2nd place)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}}2 years ({{ill|lt=580 BC|Q244713|WD=Q244713}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}|Also known as Ojinja Shrine |Fudasho, etc.= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}}3rd {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} (Orenzo) |Shinto ritual= |Map=Japan Nara }} [[Image:O-jinja 201805c.jpg|thumb|200px|Main shrine (May 2018)]] [[Image: Shrine gate of Tawaramoto Shrine Shrine gate of Ō-jinja 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Ichino Torii (November 2011)]] [[Image:O-jinja 201805b.jpg|thumb|200px|Second Torii (May 2018)]] [[Image:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja Ishi-toro.jpg|thumb|200px|A row of consecrated [[lanterns|stone lanterns]] lined up behind the second torii gate (August 2012)]]'''Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine'' jalink|Isoshi-gun|Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is a prefectural shrine. It is generally called ''Tajinja'' (Ojinja), and is also written as Tasha, Taza Shrine, Taisha, and Oosha. {{ill|lt=company name|Q11588355|WD=Q11588355}} The sign reads ``Ta{{Unicode|㘴}}Yashiritsuhiko Shrine.'' === Enshrined God === The following four gods are {{ill|lt=Enshrined Deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deities}}. * Daiichi Shrine Kamiwa Iwayohiko ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}: Father of Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto) * Second shrine {{ill|lt=Kamiyaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} (Emperor Jimmu {{ill|lt=Prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}}) * Third Shrine Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}}: younger brother of Kamiyami no Mikoto) * Fourth Shrine Himemikami ({{ill|lt=Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}: {{ill|lt=grandparents|Q167918|WD=Q167918|lt=grandmother}} of the god Yaimimi no Mikoto) In addition, {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705}}, who compiled the ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'', is also enshrined here. This area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Ta clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}, and is believed to have been dedicated to the Ta clan's {{ill|lt=tutelary deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|lt=ancestor deity}}, Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto. In the ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' it is written as ``{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Toichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}} Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2.''In {{ill|lt=Hisayasu|Q1193860|WD=Q1193860}}5 years ({{ill|lt=1149|Q19638|WD=Q19638}}), our company {{ill|lt=Negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490}}{{ill|lt=Asaomi|Q4807592|WD=Q4807592}} In the ``Tajingu Shujinsho'' submitted by Tsunemaro to the {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q1351234|WD=Q1351234}}, it lists the following two shrines, and it is also written that Mihishirihikohimenokami, based on the company's name, is the second shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyocho. * Chinko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto (Utsu no Miko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto) Imperial Statue Qiongyokuza {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)#Takayasu District|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Same deity as Kasukabe Zauzu Mikono Shrine * Amatsuoya Sakatsuhime no Mikoto Same deity as the sacred Enkyoza {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami Takaza Shrine/Iwato Shrine|Q11442907|WD=Q11442907|lt=Kasukabeza Amaterasu Ojinsha}} {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}}2 ({{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|WD=Q6993}}), it has the current four seats, but {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji|1=UNKNOWN}} era's ``Shinto Detail Book'' states that the first and fourth shrines of the main shrine are treated as {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha}}, and the two main deities enshrined are Kamiyami no Mikoto and Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto.According to the company's name, it is a shrine dedicated to Mishiritsuhiko, but this shrine is thought to refer to the god Yaimimi no Mikoto. Although he was the eldest son of Emperor Jinmu, he handed over the {{ill|lt=Imperial Throne|Q1981690|WD=Q1981690}} to his younger brother, so he is also called ``Mishiritsuhiko'' which means ``retired.'' === History === {{ill|lt=Japanese Mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|lt=Kikiki Mythology}} says, ``{{ill|lt=Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} passed on the imperial throne to his younger brother, and he himself enshrined {{ill|lt=Amatsukami/Kunitsukami|Q11442793|WD=Q11442793|lt=Jingi}},'' and this is the beginning of our company. In {{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=6th century BC|Q25296|WD=Q25296|lt=580 BC}}), Kamiyami no Mikoto built a mansion in Kasuga Prefecture (later {{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|lt=Toichi Prefecture}}). There he established {{ill|lt=Shinryō|Q60587|WD=Q60587}}{{ill|lt=Iwaza|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakyo}}, personally presided over the rituals, and enshrined the gods of Dainichi, the distant ancestor of Kasuga {{ill|lt=prefectural head|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487}}. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7th year ({{ill|lt=91 BC|Q40968|WD=Q40968}}), the shrine was remodeled and the Amatsu Nichiron Gyoku no Mikoto and Tensho Kagami Tsurugi gods were offered there.{{ill|lt=Shosoin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}'s {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}}2nd year ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=730th|1=UNKNOWN}}) {{ill|lt=Yamato Country|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Sei Tax Book|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592}} "Daikobe Inaichimangobo It is written as ``柱一萬三捌集肆笀', and it is written as ``柱一萬 連 連 連 連 連 ＧＧ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ properly The {{ill|lt=rice|Q161426|WD=Q161426|lt=rice}} owned by (Private House)|Q11589711|Kobe}} had a large economic power compared to other companies, with 10,690 bundles and 9 bundles, including 4 bundles of 138 for {{ill|lt=tax|Q847178|WD=Q847178|lt=rice tax}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," Yamato Province{{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}} It is written as ``Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2'', and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=Monthly}}, {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944|Ainame}}, {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niname}}'s {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}. {{ill|lt=Eiji|Q1194167|WD=Q1194167}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=1141|Q19613|WD=Q19613}}), in the "Draft of Notes to the Many Shrines" }}, and the {{ill|lt=plaque|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873}} of ``Shoichii {{ill|lt=Order of Honor|Q17990718|WD=Q17990718|lt=Order of First Class}} Daimyojin'' is displayed on the first {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} and the second torii. The first torii gate is located along the Terakawa River, beyond the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} tracks, about 800m east of the shrine.In ancient times it was called Kasuga Shrine, and the current shrine territory is approximately 10,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2}}, but according to "Yamato Shiryo" (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}21 ({{ill|lt=1552|Q6565|WD=Q6565}}), 6 {{jalink|towns} surrounding the local lord, {{ill|lt=Toichi Tokatsu|Q11405030|WD=Q11405030}} (Unit)|Q1185752|Town}} It is said that a square piece of land (approximately 420,000 m2) was donated, and there were torii gates on all sides. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period, it was promoted to a local shrine of {{ill|lt=Tamura|Q17174841|WD=Q17174841}}, and in {{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}12) it was promoted to a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Behind the main shrine is a small hill called ``Jinbuzuka,'' which is thought to be an ancient ritual site or {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47), {{ill|lt=Asuka River behind our company (Nara Prefecture)|Q11665966|WD=Q11665966|lt=Asuka River}} Ruins (multiple ruins) from the {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} were discovered within the precincts during embankment construction. === Precincts === * Main shrine - Four shrine shrines {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} lined up in the east and west. In {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}52), the {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} was changed to a copper plate roof. ** First Hall (designated by Nara Prefecture {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}20 ({{ill|lt=1735|Q6984|WD=Q6984}}). ** Second Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in 1735. ** Third Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in Kyoho and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} years ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892}} - {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}}). ** Fourth Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt during the Kyoho period (1716-1764). * Middle gate * Hall of worship * Tajinja Shrine Museum * Shrine office ** Shrine office front gate === Setsusha === '''Precinct shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * {{ill|lt=Ishigami Shrine|Q708859|WD=Q708859|lt=Ishigami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kama Shrine|Q60990370|WD=Q60990370}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} ``Kingaisha'''' There are the following shrines outside the precincts, and all of them are Shikinai shrines. In "Gogun Shrine Record", the two main shrines and the following four princes are called ``Yutomi Rokusho Shrine''. * Komori Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taichōshin Yasumanro}}, Shikinai Kosha/Kosha Kamimei Shrine * Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Oshijin no Mikoto, Shikinai Small Shrine/Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Himeji no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Himeji no Mikoto, Shikinai small shrine/Himeoji no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Yanari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Yanari Shrine, Shikinai Small Shrine/Yanari Shrine === Festival === The grand festival is held on the third Sunday in April and is called Ohrenzo (the festival is called Renzo in Yamato). In the era when {{ill|lt=lunar calendar|Q11511066|WD=Q11511066}} was used, the festival was held around {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268}}. === Cultural assets === === Nara Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine Main Hall 4 Buildings - {{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}} Designated on March 22, 1996 [http://www.pref.nara.jp/40279.htm List of Prefecturally Designated Cultural Properties], Nara Prefecture, as of April 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2). === Tawaramoto Town designated tangible cultural property === * Wooden {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taian Manso}}{{ill|lt=God Statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} - [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/soshki/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazai/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/10655.html Taian Manso Statue at Tajinja Shrine], which was once enshrined in the second hall of the main shrine, Tawaramoto Town. === Transportation access === * 10 minutes walk southwest from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kasanui Station|Q6374013|WD=Q6374013}}. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kasanui Village|Q11599947|WD=Q11599947}} * {{ill|lt=Danguriyama Tumulus|Q136707130|WD=Q136707130}} === References === * {{Digitized materials of the National Diet Library|950814/94|Yamato Shiryo. Volume 2}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22401 Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2], {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|Q495330|WD=Q495330}} Shrine historical materials database === External link === {{commons|Category:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja}} * [http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031401-01.html Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine] * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/3452.html Tajinja Main Hall], {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00235.html Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine (the person on the left of the seat is the mouth)], {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} Historical and Cultural Resources Database * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/route_kiki/k02/index.html To the hometown of the Ta clan that produced Taian Manyo (Kiki Route 02)], Nara Prefecture [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/index.html Nara traced in Kiki/Manyo] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Onimasu Mishiritsuhikoshinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|社おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705}} |Shaku = Shikinaisha ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}}2nd place)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}}2 years ({{ill|lt=580 BC|Q244713|WD=Q244713}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}|Also known as Ojinja Shrine |Fudasho, etc.= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}}3rd {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} (Orenzo) |Shinto ritual= |Map=Japan Nara }} [[Image:O-jinja 201805c.jpg|thumb|200px|Main shrine (May 2018)]] [[Image: Shrine gate of Tawaramoto Shrine Shrine gate of Ō-jinja 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Ichino Torii (November 2011)]] [[Image:O-jinja 201805b.jpg|thumb|200px|Second Torii (May 2018)]] [[Image:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja Ishi-toro.jpg|thumb|200px|A row of consecrated [[lanterns|stone lanterns]] lined up behind the second torii gate (August 2012)]]'''Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine'' jalink|Isoshi-gun|Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is a prefectural shrine. It is generally called ''Tajinja'' (Ojinja), and is also written as Tasha, Taza Shrine, Taisha, and Oosha. {{ill|lt=company name|Q11588355|WD=Q11588355}} The sign reads ``Ta{{Unicode|㘴}}Yashiritsuhiko Shrine.'' === Enshrined God === The following four gods are {{ill|lt=Enshrined Deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deities}}. * Daiichi Shrine Kamiwa Iwayohiko ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}: Father of Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto) * Second shrine {{ill|lt=Kamiyaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} (Emperor Jimmu {{ill|lt=Prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}}) * Third Shrine Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}}: younger brother of Kamiyami no Mikoto) * Fourth Shrine Himemikami ({{ill|lt=Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}: {{ill|lt=grandparents|Q167918|WD=Q167918|lt=grandmother}} of the god Yaimimi no Mikoto) In addition, {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705}}, who compiled the ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'', is also enshrined here. This area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Ta clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}, and is believed to have been dedicated to the Ta clan's {{ill|lt=tutelary deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|lt=ancestor deity}}, Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto. In the ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' it is written as ``{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Toichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}} Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2.''In {{ill|lt=Hisayasu|Q1193860|WD=Q1193860}}5 years ({{ill|lt=1149|Q19638|WD=Q19638}}), our company {{ill|lt=Negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490}}{{ill|lt=Asaomi|Q4807592|WD=Q4807592}} In the ``Tajingu Shujinsho'' submitted by Tsunemaro to the {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q1351234|WD=Q1351234}}, it lists the following two shrines, and it is also written that Mihishirihikohimenokami, based on the company's name, is the second shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyocho. * Chinko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto (Utsu no Miko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto) Imperial Statue Qiongyokuza {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)#Takayasu District|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Same deity as Kasukabe Zauzu Mikono Shrine * Amatsuoya Sakatsuhime no Mikoto Same deity as the sacred Enkyoza {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami Takaza Shrine/Iwato Shrine|Q11442907|WD=Q11442907|lt=Kasukabeza Amaterasu Ojinsha}} {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}}2 ({{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|WD=Q6993}}), it has the current four seats, but {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji|1=UNKNOWN}} era's ``Shinto Detail Book'' states that the first and fourth shrines of the main shrine are treated as {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha}}, and the two main deities enshrined are Kamiyami no Mikoto and Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto.According to the company's name, it is a shrine dedicated to Mishiritsuhiko, but this shrine is thought to refer to the god Yaimimi no Mikoto. Although he was the eldest son of Emperor Jinmu, he handed over the {{ill|lt=Imperial Throne|Q1981690|WD=Q1981690}} to his younger brother, so he is also called ``Mishiritsuhiko'' which means ``retired.'' === History === {{ill|lt=Japanese Mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|lt=Kikiki Mythology}} says, ``{{ill|lt=Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} passed on the imperial throne to his younger brother, and he himself enshrined {{ill|lt=Amatsukami/Kunitsukami|Q11442793|WD=Q11442793|lt=Jingi}},'' and this is the beginning of our company. In {{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=6th century BC|Q25296|WD=Q25296|lt=580 BC}}), Kamiyami no Mikoto built a mansion in Kasuga Prefecture (later {{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|lt=Toichi Prefecture}}). There he established {{ill|lt=Shinryō|Q60587|WD=Q60587}}{{ill|lt=Iwaza|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakyo}}, personally presided over the rituals, and enshrined the gods of Dainichi, the distant ancestor of Kasuga {{ill|lt=prefectural head|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487}}. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7th year ({{ill|lt=91 BC|Q40968|WD=Q40968}}), the shrine was remodeled and the Amatsu Nichiron Gyoku no Mikoto and Tensho Kagami Tsurugi gods were offered there.{{ill|lt=Shosoin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}'s {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}}2nd year ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=730th|1=UNKNOWN}}) {{ill|lt=Yamato Country|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Sei Tax Book|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592}} "Daikobe Inaichimangobo It is written as ``柱一萬三捌集肆笀', and it is written as ``柱一萬 連 連 連 連 連 ＧＧ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ properly The {{ill|lt=rice|Q161426|WD=Q161426|lt=rice}} owned by (Private House)|Q11589711|Kobe}} had a large economic power compared to other companies, with 10,690 bundles and 9 bundles, including 4 bundles of 138 for {{ill|lt=tax|Q847178|WD=Q847178|lt=rice tax}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," Yamato Province{{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}} It is written as ``Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2'', and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=Monthly}}, {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944|Ainame}}, {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niname}}'s {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}. {{ill|lt=Eiji|Q1194167|WD=Q1194167}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=1141|Q19613|WD=Q19613}}), in the "Draft of Notes to the Many Shrines" }}, and the {{ill|lt=plaque|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873}} of ``Shoichii {{ill|lt=Order of Honor|Q17990718|WD=Q17990718|lt=Order of First Class}} Daimyojin'' is displayed on the first {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} and the second torii. The first torii gate is located along the Terakawa River, beyond the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} tracks, about 800m east of the shrine.In ancient times it was called Kasuga Shrine, and the current shrine territory is approximately 10,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2}}, but according to "Yamato Shiryo" (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}21 ({{ill|lt=1552|Q6565|WD=Q6565}}), 6 {{jalink|towns} surrounding the local lord, {{ill|lt=Toichi Tokatsu|Q11405030|WD=Q11405030}} (Unit)|Q1185752|Town}} It is said that a square piece of land (approximately 420,000 m2) was donated, and there were torii gates on all sides. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period, it was promoted to a local shrine of {{ill|lt=Tamura|Q17174841|WD=Q17174841}}, and in {{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}12) it was promoted to a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Behind the main shrine is a small hill called ``Jinbuzuka,'' which is thought to be an ancient ritual site or {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47), {{ill|lt=Asuka River behind our company (Nara Prefecture)|Q11665966|WD=Q11665966|lt=Asuka River}} Ruins (multiple ruins) from the {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} were discovered within the precincts during embankment construction. === Precincts === * Main shrine - Four shrine shrines {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} lined up in the east and west. In {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}52), the {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} was changed to a copper plate roof. ** First Hall (designated by Nara Prefecture {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}20 ({{ill|lt=1735|Q6984|WD=Q6984}}). ** Second Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in 1735. ** Third Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in Kyoho and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} years ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892}} - {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}}). ** Fourth Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt during the Kyoho period (1716-1764). * Middle gate * Hall of worship * Tajinja Shrine Museum * Shrine office ** Shrine office front gate === Setsusha === '''Precinct shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * {{ill|lt=Ishigami Shrine|Q708859|WD=Q708859|lt=Ishigami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kama Shrine|Q60990370|WD=Q60990370}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} ``Kingaisha'''' There are the following shrines outside the precincts, and all of them are Shikinai shrines. In "Gogun Shrine Record", the two main shrines and the following four princes are called ``Yutomi Rokusho Shrine''. * Komori Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taichōshin Yasumanro}}, Shikinai Kosha/Kosha Kamimei Shrine * Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Oshijin no Mikoto, Shikinai Small Shrine/Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Himeji no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Himeji no Mikoto, Shikinai small shrine/Himeoji no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Yanari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Yanari Shrine, Shikinai Small Shrine/Yanari Shrine === Festival === The grand festival is held on the third Sunday in April and is called Ohrenzo (the festival is called Renzo in Yamato). In the era when {{ill|lt=lunar calendar|Q11511066|WD=Q11511066}} was used, the festival was held around {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268}}. === Cultural assets === === Nara Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine Main Hall 4 Buildings - {{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}} Designated on March 22, 1996 [http://www.pref.nara.jp/40279.htm List of Prefecturally Designated Cultural Properties], Nara Prefecture, as of April 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2). === Tawaramoto Town designated tangible cultural property === * Wooden {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taian Manso}}{{ill|lt=God Statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} - [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/soshki/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazai/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/10655.html Taian Manso Statue at Tajinja Shrine], which was once enshrined in the second hall of the main shrine, Tawaramoto Town. === Transportation access === * 10 minutes walk southwest from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kasanui Station|Q6374013|WD=Q6374013}}. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kasanui Village|Q11599947|WD=Q11599947}} * {{ill|lt=Danguriyama Tumulus|Q136707130|WD=Q136707130}} === References === * {{Digitized materials of the National Diet Library|950814/94|Yamato Shiryo. Volume 2}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22401 Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2], {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|Q495330|WD=Q495330}} Shrine historical materials database === External link === {{commons|Category:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja}} * [http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031401-01.html Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine] * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/3452.html Tajinja Main Hall], {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00235.html Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine (the person on the left of the seat is the mouth)], {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} Historical and Cultural Resources Database * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/route_kiki/k02/index.html To the hometown of the Ta clan that produced Taian Manyo (Kiki Route 02)], Nara Prefecture [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/index.html Nara traced in Kiki/Manyo] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Onimasu Mishiritsuhikoshinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|社おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

content == {{Special characters}} {{Shrine |Name=Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine |Image=[[File:O-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Isoki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} 569 Taaza Miyanouchi |Latitude degrees=34|Latitude minutes=32|Latitude seconds=08 |Longitude degrees=135|Longitude minutes=47|Longitude seconds=11 |Enshrined deity=Kamiwa Iwayohikoson ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) nk|Prince|Q12886381}})
Kanuma Kawami no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}})
Himegokami ({{ill|lt=Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}）
{{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705}} |Shaku = Shikinaisha ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}}2nd place)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}}2 years ({{ill|lt=580 BC|Q244713|WD=Q244713}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}|Also known as Ojinja Shrine |Fudasho, etc.= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}}3rd {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} (Orenzo) |Shinto ritual= |Map=Japan Nara }} [[Image:O-jinja 201805c.jpg|thumb|200px|Main shrine (May 2018)]] [[Image: Shrine gate of Tawaramoto Shrine Shrine gate of Ō-jinja 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Ichino Torii (November 2011)]] [[Image:O-jinja 201805b.jpg|thumb|200px|Second Torii (May 2018)]] [[Image:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja Ishi-toro.jpg|thumb|200px|A row of consecrated [[lanterns|stone lanterns]] lined up behind the second torii gate (August 2012)]]'''Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine'' jalink|Isoshi-gun|Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is a prefectural shrine. It is generally called ''Tajinja'' (Ojinja), and is also written as Tasha, Taza Shrine, Taisha, and Oosha. {{ill|lt=company name|Q11588355|WD=Q11588355}} The sign reads ``Ta{{Unicode|㘴}}Yashiritsuhiko Shrine.'' === Enshrined God === The following four gods are {{ill|lt=Enshrined Deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deities}}. * Daiichi Shrine Kamiwa Iwayohiko ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}: Father of Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto) * Second shrine {{ill|lt=Kamiyaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} (Emperor Jimmu {{ill|lt=Prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}}) * Third Shrine Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}}: younger brother of Kamiyami no Mikoto) * Fourth Shrine Himemikami ({{ill|lt=Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}: {{ill|lt=grandparents|Q167918|WD=Q167918|lt=grandmother}} of the god Yaimimi no Mikoto) In addition, {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705}}, who compiled the ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'', is also enshrined here. This area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Ta clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}, and is believed to have been dedicated to the Ta clan's {{ill|lt=tutelary deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|lt=ancestor deity}}, Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto. In the ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' it is written as ``{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Toichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}} Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2.''In {{ill|lt=Hisayasu|Q1193860|WD=Q1193860}}5 years ({{ill|lt=1149|Q19638|WD=Q19638}}), our company {{ill|lt=Negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490}}{{ill|lt=Asaomi|Q4807592|WD=Q4807592}} In the ``Tajingu Shujinsho'' submitted by Tsunemaro to the {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q1351234|WD=Q1351234}}, it lists the following two shrines, and it is also written that Mihishirihikohimenokami, based on the company's name, is the second shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyocho. * Chinko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto (Utsu no Miko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto) Imperial Statue Qiongyokuza {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)#Takayasu District|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Same deity as Kasukabe Zauzu Mikono Shrine * Amatsuoya Sakatsuhime no Mikoto Same deity as the sacred Enkyoza {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami Takaza Shrine/Iwato Shrine|Q11442907|WD=Q11442907|lt=Kasukabeza Amaterasu Ojinsha}} {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}}2 ({{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|WD=Q6993}}), it has the current four seats, but {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji|1=UNKNOWN}} era's ``Shinto Detail Book'' states that the first and fourth shrines of the main shrine are treated as {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha}}, and the two main deities enshrined are Kamiyami no Mikoto and Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto.According to the company's name, it is a shrine dedicated to Mishiritsuhiko, but this shrine is thought to refer to the god Yaimimi no Mikoto. Although he was the eldest son of Emperor Jinmu, he handed over the {{ill|lt=Imperial Throne|Q1981690|WD=Q1981690}} to his younger brother, so he is also called ``Mishiritsuhiko'' which means ``retired.'' === History === {{ill|lt=Japanese Mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|lt=Kikiki Mythology}} says, ``{{ill|lt=Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} passed on the imperial throne to his younger brother, and he himself enshrined {{ill|lt=Amatsukami/Kunitsukami|Q11442793|WD=Q11442793|lt=Jingi}},'' and this is the beginning of our company. In {{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=6th century BC|Q25296|WD=Q25296|lt=580 BC}}), Kamiyami no Mikoto built a mansion in Kasuga Prefecture (later {{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|lt=Toichi Prefecture}}). There he established {{ill|lt=Shinryō|Q60587|WD=Q60587}}{{ill|lt=Iwaza|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakyo}}, personally presided over the rituals, and enshrined the gods of Dainichi, the distant ancestor of Kasuga {{ill|lt=prefectural head|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487}}. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 7th year ({{ill|lt=91 BC|Q40968|WD=Q40968}}), the shrine was remodeled and the Amatsu Nichiron Gyoku no Mikoto and Tensho Kagami Tsurugi gods were offered there.{{ill|lt=Shosoin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}'s {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}}2nd year ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=730th|1=UNKNOWN}}) {{ill|lt=Yamato Country|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Sei Tax Book|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592}} "Daikobe Inaichimangobo It is written as ``柱一萬三捌集肆笀', and it is written as ``柱一萬 連 連 連 連 連 ＧＧ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ properly The {{ill|lt=rice|Q161426|WD=Q161426|lt=rice}} owned by (Private House)|Q11589711|Kobe}} had a large economic power compared to other companies, with 10,690 bundles and 9 bundles, including 4 bundles of 138 for {{ill|lt=tax|Q847178|WD=Q847178|lt=rice tax}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," Yamato Province{{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}} It is written as ``Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2'', and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=Monthly}}, {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944|Ainame}}, {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niname}}'s {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}}. {{ill|lt=Eiji|Q1194167|WD=Q1194167}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=1141|Q19613|WD=Q19613}}), in the "Draft of Notes to the Many Shrines" }}, and the {{ill|lt=plaque|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873}} of ``Shoichii {{ill|lt=Order of Honor|Q17990718|WD=Q17990718|lt=Order of First Class}} Daimyojin'' is displayed on the first {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} and the second torii. The first torii gate is located along the Terakawa River, beyond the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} tracks, about 800m east of the shrine.In ancient times it was called Kasuga Shrine, and the current shrine territory is approximately 10,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2}}, but according to "Yamato Shiryo" (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}21 ({{ill|lt=1552|Q6565|WD=Q6565}}), 6 {{jalink|towns} surrounding the local lord, {{ill|lt=Toichi Tokatsu|Q11405030|WD=Q11405030}} (Unit)|Q1185752|Town}} It is said that a square piece of land (approximately 420,000 m2) was donated, and there were torii gates on all sides. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period, it was promoted to a local shrine of {{ill|lt=Tamura|Q17174841|WD=Q17174841}}, and in {{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}12) it was promoted to a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Behind the main shrine is a small hill called ``Jinbuzuka,'' which is thought to be an ancient ritual site or {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47), {{ill|lt=Asuka River behind our company (Nara Prefecture)|Q11665966|WD=Q11665966|lt=Asuka River}} Ruins (multiple ruins) from the {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} were discovered within the precincts during embankment construction. === Precincts === * Main shrine - Four shrine shrines {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} lined up in the east and west. In {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}52), the {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} was changed to a copper plate roof. ** First Hall (designated by Nara Prefecture {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}20 ({{ill|lt=1735|Q6984|WD=Q6984}}). ** Second Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in 1735. ** Third Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in Kyoho and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} years ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892}} - {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}}). ** Fourth Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt during the Kyoho period (1716-1764). * Middle gate * Hall of worship * Tajinja Shrine Museum * Shrine office ** Shrine office front gate === Setsusha === '''Precinct shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * {{ill|lt=Ishigami Shrine|Q708859|WD=Q708859|lt=Ishigami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kama Shrine|Q60990370|WD=Q60990370}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} ``Kingaisha'''' There are the following shrines outside the precincts, and all of them are Shikinai shrines. In "Gogun Shrine Record", the two main shrines and the following four princes are called ``Yutomi Rokusho Shrine''. * Komori Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taichōshin Yasumanro}}, Shikinai Kosha/Kosha Kamimei Shrine * Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Oshijin no Mikoto, Shikinai Small Shrine/Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Himeji no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Himeji no Mikoto, Shikinai small shrine/Himeoji no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Yanari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Yanari Shrine, Shikinai Small Shrine/Yanari Shrine === Festival === The grand festival is held on the third Sunday in April and is called Ohrenzo (the festival is called Renzo in Yamato). In the era when {{ill|lt=lunar calendar|Q11511066|WD=Q11511066}} was used, the festival was held around {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268}}. === Cultural assets === === Nara Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine Main Hall 4 Buildings - {{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}} Designated on March 22, 1996 [http://www.pref.nara.jp/40279.htm List of Prefecturally Designated Cultural Properties], Nara Prefecture, as of April 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2). === Tawaramoto Town designated tangible cultural property === * Wooden {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taian Manso}}{{ill|lt=God Statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751}} - [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/soshki/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazai/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/10655.html Taian Manso Statue at Tajinja Shrine], which was once enshrined in the second hall of the main shrine, Tawaramoto Town. === Transportation access === * 10 minutes walk southwest from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kasanui Station|Q6374013|WD=Q6374013}}. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kasanui Village|Q11599947|WD=Q11599947}} * {{ill|lt=Danguriyama Tumulus|Q136707130|WD=Q136707130}} === References === * {{Digitized materials of the National Diet Library|950814/94|Yamato Shiryo. Volume 2}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22401 Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2], {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|Q495330|WD=Q495330}} Shrine historical materials database === External link === {{commons|Category:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja}} * [http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031401-01.html Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine] * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/3452.html Tajinja Main Hall], {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00235.html Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine (the person on the left of the seat is the mouth)], {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}} Historical and Cultural Resources Database * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/route_kiki/k02/index.html To the hometown of the Ta clan that produced Taian Manyo (Kiki Route 02)], Nara Prefecture [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/index.html Nara traced in Kiki/Manyo] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Onimasu Mishiritsuhikoshinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|社おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Shrine

{{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|1=Ō no Yasumaro}} |Shaku = Shikinaisha ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}}2nd place)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}2 years ({{ill|lt=580 BC|Q244713|WD=Q244713|1=580 BC}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}|Also known as Ojinja Shrine |Fudasho, etc.= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}}3rd {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} (Orenzo) |Shinto ritual= |Map=Japan Nara }} [[Image:O-jinja 201805c.jpg|thumb|200px|Main shrine (May 2018)]] [[Image: Shrine gate of Tawaramoto Shrine Shrine gate of Ō-jinja 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Ichino Torii (November 2011)]] [[Image:O-jinja 201805b.jpg|thumb|200px|Second Torii (May 2018)]] [[Image:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja Ishi-toro.jpg|thumb|200px|A row of consecrated [[lanterns|stone lanterns]] lined up behind the second torii gate (August 2012)]]'''Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine'' jalink|Isoshi-gun|Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural shrine. It is generally called ''Tajinja'' (Ojinja), and is also written as Tasha, Taza Shrine, Taisha, and Oosha. {{ill|lt=company name|Q11588355|WD=Q11588355|1=company name}} The sign reads ``Ta{{Unicode|㘴}}Yashiritsuhiko Shrine.'' === Enshrined God === The following four gods are {{ill|lt=Enshrined Deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deities|1=Saijin}}. * Daiichi Shrine Kamiwa Iwayohiko ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}: Father of Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto) * Second shrine {{ill|lt=Kamiyaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (Emperor Jimmu {{ill|lt=Prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}}) * Third Shrine Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}: younger brother of Kamiyami no Mikoto) * Fourth Shrine Himemikami ({{ill|lt=Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}: {{ill|lt=grandparents|Q167918|WD=Q167918|lt=grandmother|1=Grandparent}} of the god Yaimimi no Mikoto) In addition, {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|1=Ō no Yasumaro}}, who compiled the ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', is also enshrined here. This area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Ta clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}, and is believed to have been dedicated to the Ta clan's {{ill|lt=tutelary deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|lt=ancestor deity|1=Ujigami}}, Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto. In the ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is written as ``{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Toichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2.''In {{ill|lt=Hisayasu|Q1193860|WD=Q1193860|1=Kyūan}}5 years ({{ill|lt=1149|Q19638|WD=Q19638|1=1149}}), our company {{ill|lt=Negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|1=Negi}}{{ill|lt=Asaomi|Q4807592|WD=Q4807592|1=Ason}} In the ``Tajingu Shujinsho'' submitted by Tsunemaro to the {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q1351234|WD=Q1351234|1=Kokushi (official)}}, it lists the following two shrines, and it is also written that Mihishirihikohimenokami, based on the company's name, is the second shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyocho. * Chinko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto (Utsu no Miko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto) Imperial Statue Qiongyokuza {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)#Takayasu District|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District|1=Takayasu}} Same deity as Kasukabe Zauzu Mikono Shrine * Amatsuoya Sakatsuhime no Mikoto Same deity as the sacred Enkyoza {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami Takaza Shrine/Iwato Shrine|Q11442907|WD=Q11442907|lt=Kasukabeza Amaterasu Ojinsha|1=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine}} {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}}2 ({{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|WD=Q6993|1=1737}}), it has the current four seats, but {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji|1=UNKNOWN}} era's ``Shinto Detail Book'' states that the first and fourth shrines of the main shrine are treated as {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha|1=Setsumatsusha}}, and the two main deities enshrined are Kamiyami no Mikoto and Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto.According to the company's name, it is a shrine dedicated to Mishiritsuhiko, but this shrine is thought to refer to the god Yaimimi no Mikoto. Although he was the eldest son of Emperor Jinmu, he handed over the {{ill|lt=Imperial Throne|Q1981690|WD=Q1981690|1=Chrysanthemum Throne}} to his younger brother, so he is also called ``Mishiritsuhiko'' which means ``retired.'' === History === {{ill|lt=Japanese Mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|lt=Kikiki Mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} says, ``{{ill|lt=Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} passed on the imperial throne to his younger brother, and he himself enshrined {{ill|lt=Amatsukami/Kunitsukami|Q11442793|WD=Q11442793|lt=Jingi|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami}},'' and this is the beginning of our company. In {{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=6th century BC|Q25296|WD=Q25296|lt=580 BC|1=6th century BC}}), Kamiyami no Mikoto built a mansion in Kasuga Prefecture (later {{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|lt=Toichi Prefecture|1=Toichi district}}). There he established {{ill|lt=Shinryō|Q60587|WD=Q60587|1=Himorogi}}{{ill|lt=Iwaza|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakyo|1=Iwakura rock}}, personally presided over the rituals, and enshrined the gods of Dainichi, the distant ancestor of Kasuga {{ill|lt=prefectural head|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}}. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7th year ({{ill|lt=91 BC|Q40968|WD=Q40968|1=91 BC}}), the shrine was remodeled and the Amatsu Nichiron Gyoku no Mikoto and Tensho Kagami Tsurugi gods were offered there.{{ill|lt=Shosoin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}'s {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}}2nd year ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=730th|1=UNKNOWN}}) {{ill|lt=Yamato Country|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Sei Tax Book|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=Sei Tax Book}} "Daikobe Inaichimangobo It is written as ``柱一萬三捌集肆笀', and it is written as ``柱一萬 連 連 連 連 連 ＧＧ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ properly The {{ill|lt=rice|Q161426|WD=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} owned by (Private House)|Q11589711|Kobe}} had a large economic power compared to other companies, with 10,690 bundles and 9 bundles, including 4 bundles of 138 for {{ill|lt=tax|Q847178|WD=Q847178|lt=rice tax|1=rice tax}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}," Yamato Province{{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} It is written as ``Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2'', and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=Monthly|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944|Ainame}}, {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niname|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}'s {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}}. {{ill|lt=Eiji|Q1194167|WD=Q1194167|1=Eiji (era)}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=1141|Q19613|WD=Q19613|1=1141}}), in the "Draft of Notes to the Many Shrines" }}, and the {{ill|lt=plaque|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} of ``Shoichii {{ill|lt=Order of Honor|Q17990718|WD=Q17990718|lt=Order of First Class|1=Japanese honors system}} Daimyojin'' is displayed on the first {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and the second torii. The first torii gate is located along the Terakawa River, beyond the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} tracks, about 800m east of the shrine.In ancient times it was called Kasuga Shrine, and the current shrine territory is approximately 10,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}}, but according to "Yamato Shiryo" (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}21 ({{ill|lt=1552|Q6565|WD=Q6565|1=1552}}), 6 {{jalink|towns} surrounding the local lord, {{ill|lt=Toichi Tokatsu|Q11405030|WD=Q11405030|1=Tōchi Tōkatsu}} (Unit)|Q1185752|Town}} It is said that a square piece of land (approximately 420,000 m2) was donated, and there were torii gates on all sides. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was promoted to a local shrine of {{ill|lt=Tamura|Q17174841|WD=Q17174841|1=Ō}}, and in {{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}12) it was promoted to a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Behind the main shrine is a small hill called ``Jinbuzuka,'' which is thought to be an ancient ritual site or {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}47), {{ill|lt=Asuka River behind our company (Nara Prefecture)|Q11665966|WD=Q11665966|lt=Asuka River|1=Asuka River}} Ruins (multiple ruins) from the {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} were discovered within the precincts during embankment construction. === Precincts === * Main shrine - Four shrine shrines {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} lined up in the east and west. In {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}52), the {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} was changed to a copper plate roof. ** First Hall (designated by Nara Prefecture {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}20 ({{ill|lt=1735|Q6984|WD=Q6984|1=1735}}). ** Second Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in 1735. ** Third Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in Kyoho and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} years ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} - {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659|1=1764}}). ** Fourth Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt during the Kyoho period (1716-1764). * Middle gate * Hall of worship * Tajinja Shrine Museum * Shrine office ** Shrine office front gate === Setsusha === '''Precinct shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Ishigami Shrine|Q708859|WD=Q708859|lt=Ishigami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}} * {{ill|lt=Kama Shrine|Q60990370|WD=Q60990370|1=Kamado Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} ``Kingaisha'''' There are the following shrines outside the precincts, and all of them are Shikinai shrines. In "Gogun Shrine Record", the two main shrines and the following four princes are called ``Yutomi Rokusho Shrine''. * Komori Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taichōshin Yasumanro|1=Ō no Yasumaro}}, Shikinai Kosha/Kosha Kamimei Shrine * Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Oshijin no Mikoto, Shikinai Small Shrine/Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Himeji no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Himeji no Mikoto, Shikinai small shrine/Himeoji no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Yanari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Yanari Shrine, Shikinai Small Shrine/Yanari Shrine === Festival === The grand festival is held on the third Sunday in April and is called Ohrenzo (the festival is called Renzo in Yamato). In the era when {{ill|lt=lunar calendar|Q11511066|WD=Q11511066|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} was used, the festival was held around {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268|1=Chunfen}}. === Cultural assets === === Nara Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine Main Hall 4 Buildings - {{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070|1=1996}} Designated on March 22, 1996 [http://www.pref.nara.jp/40279.htm List of Prefecturally Designated Cultural Properties], Nara Prefecture, as of April 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2). === Tawaramoto Town designated tangible cultural property === * Wooden {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taian Manso|1=Ō no Yasumaro}}{{ill|lt=God Statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}} - [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/soshki/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazai/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/10655.html Taian Manso Statue at Tajinja Shrine], which was once enshrined in the second hall of the main shrine, Tawaramoto Town. === Transportation access === * 10 minutes walk southwest from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kasanui Station|Q6374013|WD=Q6374013|1=Kasanui Station}}. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kasanui Village|Q11599947|WD=Q11599947|1=Kasanuimura}} * {{ill|lt=Danguriyama Tumulus|Q136707130|WD=Q136707130|1=Danguriyama Tumulus}} === References === * {{Digitized materials of the National Diet Library|950814/94|Yamato Shiryo. Volume 2}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22401 Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2], {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|Q495330|WD=Q495330|1=Kokugakuin University}} Shrine historical materials database === External link === {{commons|Category:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja}} * [http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031401-01.html Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine] * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/3452.html Tajinja Main Hall], {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00235.html Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine (the person on the left of the seat is the mouth)], {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} Historical and Cultural Resources Database * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/route_kiki/k02/index.html To the hometown of the Ta clan that produced Taian Manyo (Kiki Route 02)], Nara Prefecture [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/index.html Nara traced in Kiki/Manyo] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Onimasu Mishiritsuhikoshinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|社おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|1=Ō no Yasumaro}} |Shaku = Shikinaisha ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}}2nd place)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}2 years ({{ill|lt=580 BC|Q244713|WD=Q244713|1=580 BC}}) |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}|Also known as Ojinja Shrine |Fudasho, etc.= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}}3rd {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} (Orenzo) |Shinto ritual= |Map=Japan Nara }} [[Image:O-jinja 201805c.jpg|thumb|200px|Main shrine (May 2018)]] [[Image: Shrine gate of Tawaramoto Shrine Shrine gate of Ō-jinja 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Ichino Torii (November 2011)]] [[Image:O-jinja 201805b.jpg|thumb|200px|Second Torii (May 2018)]] [[Image:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja Ishi-toro.jpg|thumb|200px|A row of consecrated [[lanterns|stone lanterns]] lined up behind the second torii gate (August 2012)]]'''Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine'' jalink|Isoshi-gun|Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is a prefectural shrine. It is generally called ''Tajinja'' (Ojinja), and is also written as Tasha, Taza Shrine, Taisha, and Oosha. {{ill|lt=company name|Q11588355|WD=Q11588355|1=company name}} The sign reads ``Ta{{Unicode|㘴}}Yashiritsuhiko Shrine.'' === Enshrined God === The following four gods are {{ill|lt=Enshrined Deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deities|1=Saijin}}. * Daiichi Shrine Kamiwa Iwayohiko ({{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}: Father of Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto) * Second shrine {{ill|lt=Kamiyaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (Emperor Jimmu {{ill|lt=Prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}}) * Third Shrine Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}: younger brother of Kamiyami no Mikoto) * Fourth Shrine Himemikami ({{ill|lt=Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}: {{ill|lt=grandparents|Q167918|WD=Q167918|lt=grandmother|1=Grandparent}} of the god Yaimimi no Mikoto) In addition, {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|1=Ō no Yasumaro}}, who compiled the ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', is also enshrined here. This area was the base of the {{ill|lt=Ta clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}, and is believed to have been dedicated to the Ta clan's {{ill|lt=tutelary deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|lt=ancestor deity|1=Ujigami}}, Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto. In the ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is written as ``{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Toichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2.''In {{ill|lt=Hisayasu|Q1193860|WD=Q1193860|1=Kyūan}}5 years ({{ill|lt=1149|Q19638|WD=Q19638|1=1149}}), our company {{ill|lt=Negi|Q11594490|WD=Q11594490|1=Negi}}{{ill|lt=Asaomi|Q4807592|WD=Q4807592|1=Ason}} In the ``Tajingu Shujinsho'' submitted by Tsunemaro to the {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q1351234|WD=Q1351234|1=Kokushi (official)}}, it lists the following two shrines, and it is also written that Mihishirihikohimenokami, based on the company's name, is the second shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyocho. * Chinko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto (Utsu no Miko Sakatsuhiko no Mikoto) Imperial Statue Qiongyokuza {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)#Takayasu District|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District|1=Takayasu}} Same deity as Kasukabe Zauzu Mikono Shrine * Amatsuoya Sakatsuhime no Mikoto Same deity as the sacred Enkyoza {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami Takaza Shrine/Iwato Shrine|Q11442907|WD=Q11442907|lt=Kasukabeza Amaterasu Ojinsha|1=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine}} {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}}2 ({{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|WD=Q6993|1=1737}}), it has the current four seats, but {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji|1=UNKNOWN}} era's ``Shinto Detail Book'' states that the first and fourth shrines of the main shrine are treated as {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha|1=Setsumatsusha}}, and the two main deities enshrined are Kamiyami no Mikoto and Kaminuma Kawami no Mikoto.According to the company's name, it is a shrine dedicated to Mishiritsuhiko, but this shrine is thought to refer to the god Yaimimi no Mikoto. Although he was the eldest son of Emperor Jinmu, he handed over the {{ill|lt=Imperial Throne|Q1981690|WD=Q1981690|1=Chrysanthemum Throne}} to his younger brother, so he is also called ``Mishiritsuhiko'' which means ``retired.'' === History === {{ill|lt=Japanese Mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|lt=Kikiki Mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} says, ``{{ill|lt=Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} passed on the imperial throne to his younger brother, and he himself enshrined {{ill|lt=Amatsukami/Kunitsukami|Q11442793|WD=Q11442793|lt=Jingi|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami}},'' and this is the beginning of our company. In {{ill|lt=Emperor Suisei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=6th century BC|Q25296|WD=Q25296|lt=580 BC|1=6th century BC}}), Kamiyami no Mikoto built a mansion in Kasuga Prefecture (later {{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|lt=Toichi Prefecture|1=Toichi district}}). There he established {{ill|lt=Shinryō|Q60587|WD=Q60587|1=Himorogi}}{{ill|lt=Iwaza|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakyo|1=Iwakura rock}}, personally presided over the rituals, and enshrined the gods of Dainichi, the distant ancestor of Kasuga {{ill|lt=prefectural head|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}}. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7th year ({{ill|lt=91 BC|Q40968|WD=Q40968|1=91 BC}}), the shrine was remodeled and the Amatsu Nichiron Gyoku no Mikoto and Tensho Kagami Tsurugi gods were offered there.{{ill|lt=Shosoin Documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}'s {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}}2nd year ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=730th|1=UNKNOWN}}) {{ill|lt=Yamato Country|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Sei Tax Book|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=Sei Tax Book}} "Daikobe Inaichimangobo It is written as ``柱一萬三捌集肆笀', and it is written as ``柱一萬 連 連 連 連 連 ＧＧ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ Ｇ properly The {{ill|lt=rice|Q161426|WD=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} owned by (Private House)|Q11589711|Kobe}} had a large economic power compared to other companies, with 10,690 bundles and 9 bundles, including 4 bundles of 138 for {{ill|lt=tax|Q847178|WD=Q847178|lt=rice tax|1=rice tax}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}," Yamato Province{{ill|lt=Toichi-gun|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} It is written as ``Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2'', and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=Monthly|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944|Ainame}}, {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niname|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}'s {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}}. {{ill|lt=Eiji|Q1194167|WD=Q1194167|1=Eiji (era)}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=1141|Q19613|WD=Q19613|1=1141}}), in the "Draft of Notes to the Many Shrines" }}, and the {{ill|lt=plaque|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} of ``Shoichii {{ill|lt=Order of Honor|Q17990718|WD=Q17990718|lt=Order of First Class|1=Japanese honors system}} Daimyojin'' is displayed on the first {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and the second torii. The first torii gate is located along the Terakawa River, beyond the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} tracks, about 800m east of the shrine.In ancient times it was called Kasuga Shrine, and the current shrine territory is approximately 10,000 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}}, but according to "Yamato Shiryo" (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}21 ({{ill|lt=1552|Q6565|WD=Q6565|1=1552}}), 6 {{jalink|towns} surrounding the local lord, {{ill|lt=Toichi Tokatsu|Q11405030|WD=Q11405030|1=Tōchi Tōkatsu}} (Unit)|Q1185752|Town}} It is said that a square piece of land (approximately 420,000 m2) was donated, and there were torii gates on all sides. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was promoted to a local shrine of {{ill|lt=Tamura|Q17174841|WD=Q17174841|1=Ō}}, and in {{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}12) it was promoted to a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Behind the main shrine is a small hill called ``Jinbuzuka,'' which is thought to be an ancient ritual site or {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}47), {{ill|lt=Asuka River behind our company (Nara Prefecture)|Q11665966|WD=Q11665966|lt=Asuka River|1=Asuka River}} Ruins (multiple ruins) from the {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} to {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} were discovered within the precincts during embankment construction. === Precincts === * Main shrine - Four shrine shrines {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} lined up in the east and west. In {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}52), the {{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} was changed to a copper plate roof. ** First Hall (designated by Nara Prefecture {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}20 ({{ill|lt=1735|Q6984|WD=Q6984|1=1735}}). ** Second Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in 1735. ** Third Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt in Kyoho and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} years ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} - {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659|1=1764}}). ** Fourth Hall (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Nara Prefecture) - Rebuilt during the Kyoho period (1716-1764). * Middle gate * Hall of worship * Tajinja Shrine Museum * Shrine office ** Shrine office front gate === Setsusha === '''Precinct shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Ishigami Shrine|Q708859|WD=Q708859|lt=Ishigami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}} * {{ill|lt=Kama Shrine|Q60990370|WD=Q60990370|1=Kamado Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} ``Kingaisha'''' There are the following shrines outside the precincts, and all of them are Shikinai shrines. In "Gogun Shrine Record", the two main shrines and the following four princes are called ``Yutomi Rokusho Shrine''. * Komori Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taichōshin Yasumanro|1=Ō no Yasumaro}}, Shikinai Kosha/Kosha Kamimei Shrine * Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Oshijin no Mikoto, Shikinai Small Shrine/Ojijin no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Himeji no Mikoto Shrine - Enshrined deity: Himeji no Mikoto, Shikinai small shrine/Himeoji no Mikoto Shrine (Ronsha) * Yanari Shrine - Enshrined deity: Yanari Shrine, Shikinai Small Shrine/Yanari Shrine === Festival === The grand festival is held on the third Sunday in April and is called Ohrenzo (the festival is called Renzo in Yamato). In the era when {{ill|lt=lunar calendar|Q11511066|WD=Q11511066|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} was used, the festival was held around {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268|1=Chunfen}}. === Cultural assets === === Nara Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine Main Hall 4 Buildings - {{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070|1=1996}} Designated on March 22, 1996 [http://www.pref.nara.jp/40279.htm List of Prefecturally Designated Cultural Properties], Nara Prefecture, as of April 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2). === Tawaramoto Town designated tangible cultural property === * Wooden {{ill|lt=Taian Manso|Q384705|WD=Q384705|lt=Taian Manso|1=Ō no Yasumaro}}{{ill|lt=God Statue|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}} - [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/soshki/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazai/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/10655.html Taian Manso Statue at Tajinja Shrine], which was once enshrined in the second hall of the main shrine, Tawaramoto Town. === Transportation access === * 10 minutes walk southwest from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kasanui Station|Q6374013|WD=Q6374013|1=Kasanui Station}}. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kasanui Village|Q11599947|WD=Q11599947|1=Kasanuimura}} * {{ill|lt=Danguriyama Tumulus|Q136707130|WD=Q136707130|1=Danguriyama Tumulus}} === References === * {{Digitized materials of the National Diet Library|950814/94|Yamato Shiryo. Volume 2}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22401 Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine 2], {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|Q495330|WD=Q495330|1=Kokugakuin University}} Shrine historical materials database === External link === {{commons|Category:Oonimasumishiritsuhiko-jinja}} * [http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031401-01.html Tazaya Shiritsuhiko Shrine] * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/shitei/3452.html Tajinja Main Hall], {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00235.html Tazami Shiritsuhiko Shrine (the person on the left of the seat is the mouth)], {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} Historical and Cultural Resources Database * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/route_kiki/k02/index.html To the hometown of the Ta clan that produced Taian Manyo (Kiki Route 02)], Nara Prefecture [http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/aruku/kikimanyo/index.html Nara traced in Kiki/Manyo] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Onimasu Mishiritsuhikoshinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|社おおにますみしりつひこしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
